site.fullName: 1st IEEE International Conference on Smart Connectivity and Computational Intelligence 2027
site.organizers: Department of Computer Science Engineering and the Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering, Echelon Institute of Technology, Faridabad

hero.title: ICSCCI 2027
hero.titleLong: International Conference on Smart Connectivity and Computational Intelligence
hero.subtitle: The 1st IEEE International Conference on Smart Connectivity and Computational Intelligence 2027 (ICSCCI 2027), technically co-sponsored by the IEEE Delhi Section, will be dedicated to advancing research and innovation at the intersection of intelligent communication systems and computational intelligence. It is jointly being organized by the Department of Computer Science Engineering and the Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering at Echelon Institute of Technology, Faridabad. The conference aims to bring together leading academicians, researchers, industry professionals, and students to exchange knowledge and explore emerging technological trends.

about.heading: About the Conference
paragraphs: The 1st IEEE International Conference on Smart Connectivity and Computational Intelligence 2027 (ICSCCI 2027), technically co-sponsored by the IEEE Delhi Section, will be dedicated to advancing research and innovation at the intersection of intelligent communication systems and computational intelligence. It is jointly being organized by the Department of Computer Science Engineering and the Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering at Echelon Institute of Technology, Faridabad.
The conference aims to bring together leading academicians, researchers, industry professionals, and students to exchange knowledge and explore emerging technological trends.

ICSCCI 2027 focuses on the integration of next-generation connectivity technologies such as 5G/6G networks and the Internet of Things (IoT) with advanced computational intelligence techniques, including machine learning and deep learning. The conference will address critical challenges related to scalability, latency, reliability, security, and optimization in smart and autonomous systems. By fostering interdisciplinary collaboration between academia and industry, ICSCCI 2027 invites authors to submit original, unpublished research through the Microsoft Conference Management Toolkit, promoting innovative thinking and practical applications that contribute to the development of intelligent, sustainable, and future-ready digital infrastructures for smart societies.

institute.heading: Echelon Institute of Technology (EIT), Faridabad
institute.description: Established in 2007, EIT is a premier institution dedicated to academic excellence, innovation, and holistic development. EIT is affiliated with Guru Gobind Singh Indraprastha University, Delhi, and is approved by AICTE, Ministry of Education, and Government of India. At EIT, we strive to nurture technical and managerial leaders who drive change and innovation through a culture of inspiration, exploration, and continuous learning. We place a strong emphasis on experiential and outcome-based education, providing students with the practical skills and knowledge necessary to thrive in an ever-evolving global landscape.

departments.name: Department of Computer Science and Engineering
departments.description: Enriched with competent and well-qualified faculty members to bring out the best in students. The department holds NBA Accreditation up to Session 2027-28. Since its inception in 2007, the Department has maintained a high growth rate and a strong dedication to providing engineering education, with state-of-the-art laboratories in Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, Data Science, Cloud Computing, Cyber Security, IoT, and Robotics. Industry-oriented training, seminars, workshops, expert lectures, hackathons, corporate interactions, and Personality Development Programs (PDP) round out students' technical and professional skills.

departments.name: Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering
departments.description: Established in 2007, the ECE department is committed to academic excellence, innovation, and industry-oriented learning. Supported by experienced faculty and modern infrastructure, it offers hands-on training through advanced laboratories such as the Advanced Drone Lab, IoT, Robotics & Electronics Lab, Embedded System Design & Simulation Lab, and Advanced Communication Lab, with a strong focus on emerging technologies, research, skill development, and industry collaboration.

tracks.heading: Conference Tracks
tracks.intro: ICSCCI 2027 invites original, unpublished research contributions across the following six tracks.

items.title: Smart Communication Systems and Connectivity
topics: Satellite, Non-Terrestrial, UAV and RIS-assisted Communications
Communication Protocols, Architectures, QoS and QoE
AI-Native Networks and Intelligent Communication Networks
Signal Processing for Advanced Wireless Communications

items.title: Computational Intelligence, Data Analytics and Agentic AI
topics: Machine Learning, Deep Learning and Reinforcement Learning
Foundation Models, Large Language Models (LLMs) and Generative AI (GAI)
Pattern Recognition and Intelligent Decision Systems
Agentic AI and Autonomous Intelligent Agents

items.title: Internet of Things (IoT), Edge and Cloud Computing
topics: Internet of Things (IoT) and Industrial IoT (IIoT)
Edge Intelligence, Edge AI and Tiny ML
Smart Sensors, Embedded Systems and Connected Devices

items.title: Cybersecurity, Privacy and Trustworthy Computing
topics: Network and Information Security and Cybersecurity
AI Security, Privacy-Preserving AI, and Adversarial ML

items.title: Intelligent Automation, Optimization and Control
topics: Digital Manufacturing and Smart Process Control

items.title: Smart Applications and Emerging Technologies
topics: AI Applications in Healthcare, Finance and Education
Quantum Computing and Quantum Machine Learning
Green, Sustainable Computing and Future Digital Ecosystems

importantDates.heading: Important Dates
importantDates.note: All dates are as published; check this page for updates as the conference approaches.

callForPapers.heading: Call for Papers
callForPapers.intro: ICSCCI 2027 invites authors to submit original, unpublished research through the Microsoft Conference Management Toolkit (Microsoft CMT), promoting innovative thinking and practical applications that contribute to intelligent, sustainable, and future-ready digital infrastructures for smart societies.
guidelines: Papers must be written in English, 4–6 printed pages (10pt font), double-column IEEE conference format.
Submit original, unpublished work in PDF/MS Word format via Microsoft CMT.
Papers already submitted to other conferences/journals are not allowed.
Figures/graphs/plots must be of good resolution (600 dpi or more); tables must not be in pictorial format.
Do not use academic/social titles (Mr., Ms., Dr., Prof.) or positions (research scholar, professor, IEEE member, etc.) before author names or in affiliations.
Mention full address, affiliation, and email of all authors, and specify a corresponding author.
At least one author of an accepted paper must register and present the paper at the conference.
Authors must register their email with Microsoft CMT to submit papers.
Similarity/plagiarism score must not exceed 15%, per IEEE plagiarism guidelines.

plagiarism.heading: Policy on Plagiarism
plagiarism.text: Authors are requested to kindly refrain from plagiarism in any form. The similarity score of the paper should not be more than 15%.

callForPapers.acknowledgement: The Microsoft CMT service was used for managing the peer-reviewing process for this conference. This service was provided for free by Microsoft, who bore all expenses, including costs for Azure cloud services as well as software development and support.

paperSubmission.heading: Paper Submission
paperSubmission.intro: All papers submitted to the conference should be written in English with a maximum page length of 4–6 printed pages (10pt font) in double-column IEEE conference format.
guidelines: Submit original, unpublished work in PDF/MS Word format via Microsoft CMT.
Papers already submitted to other conferences/journals are not allowed.
Figures/graphs/plots must be good resolution (600 dpi+); tables must not be pictorial.
Do not use titles (Mr., Ms., Dr., Prof.) or positions before author names or in affiliations.
Mention full address, affiliation, and email of all authors; specify a corresponding author.
Acceptance decisions are at the discretion of the Technical Program Committee.
At least one author of an accepted paper must register and present at the conference.
Authors must register their email with Microsoft CMT to submit papers.
